cvrf:DocumentPublisher.cvrf:IssuingAuthority: The Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC) identifies, monitors, resolves, and responds to security incidents and Microsoft software security vulnerabilities. For more information, see http://www.microsoft.com/security/msrc.

cvrf:DocumentNotes.cvrf:Note: The information provided in the Microsoft Knowledge Base is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. Microsoft disclaims all warranties, either express or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose. In no event shall Microsoft Corporation or its suppliers be liable for any damages whatsoever including direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, loss of business profits or special damages, even if Microsoft Corporation or its suppliers have been advised of the possibility of such damages. Some states do not allow the exclusion or limitation of liability for consequential or incidental damages so the foregoing limitation may not apply.

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: net: rds: clear i_sends on setup unwind
drm/virtio: Fix driver removal with disabled KMS
ARM: 9475/1: entry: use byte load for KASAN VMAP stack shadow
drm/amd/display: Use krealloc_array() in dal_vector_reserve()
signal: clear JOBCTL_PENDING_MASK for caller in zap_other_threads()
netfilter: nf_conntrack: destroy stale expectfn expectations on unregister
Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling in Elasticsearch Leading to Denial of Service
vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:CWE: Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: Dhcpcd: dhcpcd infinite loop and out-of-bounds read via zero-length ipv6 nd option in router advertisement handling
vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:CWE: Loop with Unreachable Exit Condition ('Infinite Loop')

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: arm64: errata: Mitigate TLBI errata on various Arm CPUs
runc: Malicious image with /dev symlink can trigger limited host filesystem integrity violations
KVM: Don't WARN if memory is dirtied without a vCPU when the VM is dying
slimbus: qcom-ngd-ctrl: Register callbacks after creating the ngd
nvmem: layouts: onie-tlv: fix hang on unknown types
debugobjects: Do not fill_pool() if pi_blocked_on
net: bonding: fix NULL pointer dereference in bond_do_ioctl()
drm/i915/gem: Fix phys BO pread/pwrite with offset
hsr: Remove WARN_ONCE() in hsr_addr_is_self().
i2c: qcom-cci: Fix NULL pointer dereference in cci_remove()
Uncontrolled Resource Consumption in Elasticsearch Leading to Denial of Service
Bluetooth: fix UAF in l2cap_sock_cleanup_listen() vs l2cap_conn_del()
Microsoft Entra Provisioning Service Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability

vuln:Notes.vuln:Note: Server-side request forgery (ssrf) in Microsoft Entra Provisioning Service (SyncFabric) allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

Why are there no links to an update or instructions with steps that must be taken to protect from this vulnerability?

This vulnerability has already been fully mitigated by Microsoft. There is no action for users of this service to take. The purpose of this CVE is to provide further transparency.

Please see Toward greater transparency: Unveiling Cloud Service CVEs for more information.

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: Azure OpenAI Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability

vuln:Notes.vuln:Note: Server-side request forgery (ssrf) in Azure OpenAI allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

Why are there no links to an update or instructions with steps that must be taken to protect from this vulnerability?

This vulnerability has already been fully mitigated by Microsoft. There is no action for users of this service to take. The purpose of this CVE is to provide further transparency.

Please see Toward greater transparency: Unveiling Cloud Service CVEs for more information.

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: Microsoft Azure Synapse Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability

vuln:Notes.vuln:Note: Improper access control in Azure Synapse allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

Why are there no links to an update or instructions with steps that must be taken to protect from this vulnerability?

This vulnerability has already been fully mitigated by Microsoft. There is no action for users of this service to take. The purpose of this CVE is to provide further transparency.

Please see Toward greater transparency: Unveiling Cloud Service CVEs for more information.

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability

vuln:Notes.vuln:Note: Url redirection to untrusted site ('open redirect') in M365 Copilot allows an unauthorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

Why are there no links to an update or instructions with steps that must be taken to protect from this vulnerability?

This vulnerability has already been fully mitigated by Microsoft. There is no action for users of this service to take. The purpose of this CVE is to provide further transparency.

Please see Toward greater transparency: Unveiling Cloud Service CVEs for more information.

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:CWE: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

vuln:Vulnerability.vuln:Title: Microsoft Exchange Online Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability

vuln:Notes.vuln:Note: Incorrect authorization in Microsoft Exchange Online allows an authorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

Why are there no links to an update or instructions with steps that must be taken to protect from this vulnerability?

This vulnerability has already been fully mitigated by Microsoft. There is no action for users of this service to take. The purpose of this CVE is to provide further transparency.

Please see Toward greater transparency: Unveiling Cloud Service CVEs for more information.
